finding: The user runs the exact same dotfiles sync command ('Check /home/jonathan/repos/dotfiles for any uncommitted changes, git add, commit, push') verbatim across at least 4 separate sessions over 2 days. A repo-autosync skill exists and was eventually set up, but the repeated manual invocations before that point — and a lingering old-path cron that kept re-appearing — suggest the automation is not yet fully reliable or the user still defaults to manual sync out of habit. Goal: keep dotfiles repo in sync with actual dotfile state, automatically. Surface of recurrence: across sessions, across days.
existing_mitigation: repo-autosync skill exists and was applied to dotfiles. A cron job was set up but had path issues (lowercase 'repos' vs 'Repos'). The user eventually created a generalized skill for this, but manual invocations continued after setup.

finding: The user manually asks Claude to find large files in /tmp, clean up stale ONNX exports, identify pip cache bloat, and audit disk usage across at least two separate sessions. Each time, the same pattern: 'disk is packed, what are the hogs?' followed by manual triage. Goal: keep disk usage under control by periodically cleaning known-safe ephemeral locations (pip cache, stale venvs, /tmp accumulation, model exports). Surface of recurrence: across sessions, likely recurring as projects accumulate artifacts.
existing_mitigation: None found. No cron or hook for disk hygiene.

finding: The user makes repeated small behavioral adjustments to autodoro across multiple sessions: adding process exclusions (whisper-writer), changing block screen behavior (don't auto-dismiss, add Open button, pause noise during block), and tweaking break countdown UI. Each tweak requires a full Claude session to implement code changes. The user also explicitly asked for a config file to make autodoro more configurable for sharing. Goal: adjust autodoro behavior without needing code changes each time. Surface of recurrence: same project, across sessions, across days.
existing_mitigation: User explicitly requested a config file in session 9a044ead. A config.json was added, but the scope of what's configurable may not cover all the behavioral tweaks that keep coming up (block screen behavior, UI styling).

finding: The user manually evaluated whisper transcription models for speed vs quality (trying distil-small, asking about beam size, comparing options). The user then explicitly asked for a cron-based Claude agent to monitor for better model options and notify when upgrades are available. Goal: stay on the best available local transcription model without manual research. Surface of recurrence: each time a new model is released or performance is unsatisfactory. The user already proposed the automation themselves — the proposal folder was created but the agent is not yet built.
existing_mitigation: User created a proposals folder with the idea for an automated model-checker agent, but it has not been implemented yet.

finding: The user tracks security hardening across at least 3 sessions over 2 days, manually copying research notes between locations, asking Claude to verify LLM Guard setup, and maintaining a mental checklist of remaining attack surfaces. Each session picks up where the last left off by re-explaining context. Goal: systematically harden Claude Code security setup with a persistent, checkable list of mitigations and remaining gaps. Surface of recurrence: across sessions, across days, ongoing as new attack surfaces are discovered.
existing_mitigation: A research file exists at the marketplace repo (prompt-injection-security.md), and was copied to ~/.claude/. LLM Guard hooks are configured. But there is no structured checklist tracking what is done vs pending, causing repeated re-discovery of the same gaps across sessions.
